front: What is the primary objective of the identification stage in time series modeling?
back: To specify a tentative model for the time series data by determining the appropriate orders (p, d, q) for the ARIMA model, based on patterns observed in the autocorrelation and partial autocorrelation functions.

front: What is the first key objective in model identification regarding the series itself?
back: To determine if the time series is stationary. This involves checking for a constant mean and variance over time. If the series is non-stationary, the objective is to identify the appropriate degree of differencing (d) to achieve stationarity.

front: What patterns in the ACF and PACF plots are used to identify the order of an AR(p) process?
back: For an AR(p) process, the ACF decays exponentially or sinusoidally (tails off), while the PACF has a sharp cutoff after lag p (significant spikes only up to lag p).

front: What patterns in the ACF and PACF plots are used to identify the order of an MA(q) process?
back: For an MA(q) process, the ACF has a sharp cutoff after lag q (significant spikes only up to lag q), while the PACF decays exponentially or sinusoidally (tails off).

front: Why is the identification stage considered tentative?
back: Because the initial model chosen based on ACF/PACF patterns is only a preliminary guess. The final model is confirmed and validated in the subsequent stages of estimation and diagnostic checking.
